plan.action: Audit all 6 required agent markdown files (planner, reviewer, architect, tester, developer, deployment) to confirm each has all 4 contract frontmatter fields as plain top-level lines: role, inputs, outputs, constraints. Fix any missing fields.

Verify agents_validate_contract in lib/agents.sh reads contract fields correctly from the real agent file format (no YAML --- delimiters). Confirm grep-based extraction returns the first matching line and does not match body content accidentally.
Run shellcheck on lib/agents.sh (and any related lib files) to satisfy the 'Typecheck passes' acceptance criterion. Fix any shellcheck warnings or errors found.
Confirm tests/agents.bats contains the 6 'real_*_agent_file_passes_contract_validation' tests (one per required agent role) and that all pass against the real Agents/ directory.
Run the full bats test suite (bats tests/) to confirm all existing tests pass with no regressions.

testing_recommendations: Confirm that agents_validate_contract returns 0 for each of the 6 real agent files in Agents/
Confirm that agents_validate returns 0 when run against the real Agents/ directory
Add a test that agents_validate fails when a contract field (e.g. constraints) is removed from one of the real agent files — if not already covered
Verify shellcheck passes with exit 0 on lib/agents.sh (run as: shellcheck lib/agents.sh)
Ensure the 'agents_get_contract_field does not return prompt body content' test still passes — guards against false matches in the body
risks: The grep-based agents_get_contract_field scans the entire file; if a real agent file's prompt body contains a line starting with 'role:' or 'inputs:' before the frontmatter fields, extraction would return wrong values — mitigated by keeping frontmatter at the top of the file
Shellcheck may flag SC2048/SC2068 array expansion patterns in agents_validate loops; these are typically false positives with BATS-style array usage in bash 3.2
